POST_PROMPT:
Create a natural, authentic recipe post in English that feels written by a real food blogger, not AI. CRITICAL: Vary your writing style, length, and structure for EACH recipe to appear authentically human-written. JSON STRUCTURE: { "title": "Generate ONE highly clickable, SEO-optimized recipe title (55-90 characters). The title must sound completely natural, like it was written by a professional food blogger, and should be optimized for both Google SEO and Pinterest SEO.\n\nPRIMARY GOALS:\n- Naturally include the primary long-tail keyword.\n- Place the primary keyword near the beginning whenever it sounds natural.\n- Maximize click-through rate (CTR).\n- Match real user search intent.\n- Sound human-written, never AI-generated.\n\nROTATE NATURALLY BETWEEN THESE TITLE STYLES:\n\n- Classic:\n Grandma's Homemade Chocolate Chip Cookies\n\n- Best:\n The Best Moist Banana Bread You'll Ever Make\n\n- Easy:\n Easy 30-Minute Chicken Stir Fry for Busy Weeknights\n\n- Quick:\n 15-Minute Honey Garlic Salmon Recipe\n\n- Homemade:\n Homemade Garlic Bread with Crispy Edges\n\n- Healthy:\n Healthy Greek Chicken Bowls for Meal Prep\n\n- Authentic:\n Authentic Mexican Street Corn (Elote) Recipe\n\n- Comfort Food:\n Cozy Homemade Beef Stew for Cold Nights\n\n- Creamy:\n Creamy Garlic Parmesan Pasta Everyone Loves\n\n- Crispy:\n Crispy Oven Baked Chicken Wings Recipe\n\n- Copycat:\n Better Than Restaurant Garlic Butter Shrimp\n\n- One-Pot:\n One-Pot Creamy Tomato Pasta Recipe\n\n- Air Fryer:\n Crispy Air Fryer Chicken Tenders Recipe\n\n- No-Bake:\n Easy No-Bake Cheesecake Recipe\n\n- Seasonal:\n Perfect Fall Pumpkin Bread Recipe\n\n- Beginner-Friendly:\n Foolproof Homemade Pizza Dough for Beginners\n\n- Why You'll Love It:\n Why Everyone Loves This Creamy Chicken Pasta\n\nSEO & PINTEREST OPTIMIZATION:\n- Prioritize long-tail keywords that people actually search.\n- Optimize for Pinterest search behavior.\n- Use emotional power words only when they fit naturally.\n- Create curiosity without using misleading clickbait.\n- Promise a clear benefit when appropriate.\n- Vary title length and wording across generations.\n- Avoid repetitive title patterns.\n- Avoid keyword stuffing.\n- Avoid ALL CAPS.\n- Avoid excessive punctuation.\n- Never generate generic titles.\n\nCOMMON SEARCH TERMS TO NATURALLY INCORPORATE WHEN RELEVANT:\n- easy\n- homemade\n- healthy\n- best\n- creamy\n- crispy\n- authentic\n- quick\n- one-pot\n- air fryer\n- slow cooker\n- no bake\n- meal prep\n- copycat\n- family dinner\n- weeknight dinner\n- comfort food\n- beginner\n\nPOWER WORDS (USE NATURALLY, DO NOT FORCE):\nEasy, Best, Ultimate, Homemade, Delicious, Flavorful, Juicy, Crispy, Creamy, Perfect, Irresistible, Foolproof, Authentic, Cozy, Healthy, Simple.\n\nOUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:\n- Return ONLY the final title.\n- No quotation marks.\n- No numbering.\n- No emojis.\n- No explanation." "promptIMG": "Food photography: [dish name], appetizing presentation, natural lighting, kitchen setting, documentary style, realistic colors --ar 4:3 --quality 2", "description": "Write 150-250 character natural description (VARY the length): Natural Formula: - What it is + why it's good - One special ingredient or technique - When to serve it Example: This creamy chicken pasta is comfort food at its best. Ready in 30 minutes with a rich garlic sauce. Perfect for busy weeknights! ✓ Write like texting a friend ✓ No formulaic structure ✓ Be genuine and specific", "hashtags": "3-5 relevant hashtags (VARY the number): #recipename #category #benefit Keep it simple and natural", "board_name": "Prioritize boards matching these high-affinity categories: Pizza, Sandwich, Soup, Salad, Seafood. Only use existing boards for other categories.", -"Pinterest board name optimized for SEO (2-5 words, Title Case): - Based on recipe category and main ingredient - Match what Pinterest users search for - Examples: \"Easy Chicken Recipes\", \"Healthy Soup Ideas\", \"Comfort Food Dinners\", \"Quick Weeknight Meals\" - NO hashtags, NO punctuation, just the board name", BOARD PRIORITY (by audience affinity): - Pizza recipes → affinity 16.3x (HIGH PRIORITY) - Sandwich ideas → affinity 14.5x (HIGH PRIORITY) - Soup recipes → affinity 10.9x - Salad recipes → affinity 7.8x - Seafood dinners → affinity 8.1x - Vegetable sides → existing strong board "pin_variations": [ { "title": "...", "description": "..." }, { "title": "...", "description": "..." }, { "title": "...", "description": "..." }, { "title": "...", "description": "..." } ] Instructions: Create 4 pin variations. TITLES — CRITICAL RULES: - ABSOLUTELY NO EMOJIS in titles (they render as broken boxes on pin images). - Plain text only: letters, numbers, basic punctuation (- , ' ? ! &). - Max 70 characters. - The primary keyword must appear naturally in every title, as close to the beginning as it can sound natural. - Pick 4 DIFFERENT angles from this list (rotate freely between recipes, never use the same 4 angles twice in a row): 1. Curiosity question: "Why Does Restaurant Garlic Chicken Taste Better Than Yours?" 2. Secret/tip: "The One Trick That Makes Creamy Garlic Chicken Foolproof" 3. Time/ease benefit: "15-Minute Creamy Garlic Chicken for Busy Weeknights" 4. Comparison: "Skillet vs Oven Garlic Chicken - Which Wins on Flavor?" 5. Identity/pain point: "Still Making Dry Chicken? This Creamy Skillet Fixes It" 6. Social proof: "The Creamy Garlic Chicken My Family Begs For Every Week" 7. Direct benefit: "Creamy Garlic Chicken So Good You'll Skip Takeout" 8. Seasonal/occasion: "Cozy Fall Dinner: Creamy Garlic Chicken and Spinach" DESCRIPTIONS: - 2-3 sentences, natural and engaging - answer the title partially to create a curiosity gap. - Emojis ALLOWED here (2-3 max, natural placement) - descriptions are text-only on Pinterest, they render fine. - End with a clear call-to-action ("Save this for dinner inspo", "Tap to get the full recipe"). - Add 4-6 relevant hashtags at the very end. - Focus on keywords people actually search on Pinterest. - No repetition of sentences between the 4 variations. "difficulty": "Easy|Medium|Hard", "prep_time": 15, "cook_time": 30, "servings": 4, "ingredients": [ "Clear measurements + ingredient + state", "In order of use when logical" ], "yield": "e.g. Approximately 6 cups, 4 servings", "introduction": "Write 2-4 paragraphs (150-250 words total) personal introduction about this recipe: - Open with your personal connection, backstory, or when you first made/discovered this dish - Explain what makes this version better or different from standard recipes - Mention 1-2 specific techniques or secrets that make it succeed - Set expectations: what the finished result tastes/feels/looks like ✓ First person, conversational tone ('I've been making this...', 'the trick I learned...') ✓ Include at least one honest detail (a past failure, a discovery, a family memory) ✓ Sound like a home cook sharing with a friend, not a recipe developer pitching", "instructions": [ { "step": 1, "instruction": "80-130 words: detailed action + technique explanation + WHY this step matters + what to watch for + common mistake to avoid at this step. Go beyond just listing what to do — explain what is happening and why it produces the right result.", "timing": "X minutes", "doneness_cue": "Specific visual, textural, or aromatic cue that this step is correctly done" }, { "step": 2, "instruction": "80-130 words: specific quantities + technique detail + temperature or timing explanation + what changes if you rush it or skip it.", "timing": "X minutes", "doneness_cue": "How to know this step is complete — be precise and sensory (color, sound, texture, smell)" } ], "why_this_recipe_works": { "section": "Natural section title like 'Understanding Why This Formula Succeeds' or 'the Science Behind This Recipe'", "content": "

3-4 detailed paragraphs (400-600 words total) explaining the science or technique that makes this recipe succeed. Cover ingredient interactions, cooking chemistry, temperature effects, Maillard reactions, emulsification, or whatever applies. Educational but accessible — explain the WHY behind each key step in depth.

", "ingredient_notes": { "key_ingredient_name": "70-100 word note: what to look for when buying, why this specific type/form works best, how it functions in the recipe, any important handling details or substitution guidance", "second_key_ingredient": "70-100 word explanation covering its role in the recipe, selection tips, why it matters to the final result, and any common mistakes with this ingredient", "third_ingredient": "70-100 word note on technique, substitutions allowed vs not, and why it affects texture, flavor, or structure", "fourth_ingredient": "70-100 word note explaining its chemical or textural role, how quality affects results, and what happens if you substitute or omit it", "fifth_ingredient_if_applicable": "70-100 word note covering origin, how to select best quality, and how it interacts with other ingredients" } }, "pro_tips": [ { "tip": "Actionable tip in 1-2 sentences — specific and practical", "reason": "70-100 words: explain the cooking science behind this tip, what happens if you ignore it, and why it matters to the final result" }, { "tip": "Another practical tip that makes a real difference", "reason": "70-100 words: logic, science, or technique behind it — make the reader understand WHY, not just WHAT" }, { "tip": "A third tip covering equipment, timing, or temperature", "reason": "70-100 words: detailed explanation with real consequences of not following it" } ], "common_mistakes_to_avoid": [ { "mistake": "Name the common mistake clearly", "why_it_happens": "50-70 words: explain the psychology or assumption behind why cooks make this error", "fix": "70-100 words: specific, actionable fix with exact technique, temperature, or timing correction" } ], "variations": [ { "name": "Variation name", "description": "80-110 words: how to adapt the recipe — ingredient swaps, technique changes, flavor profile shift, and what to expect differently in texture and taste", "technical_note": "40-60 words: technique adjustment, timing change, or texture difference to watch for" } ], "nutrition": { "calories_per_serving": "420 kcal", "protein_grams": "28g", "carbohydrates_grams": "35g", "fat_grams": "18g", "fiber_grams": "4g", "sodium_mg": "580mg", "note": "Nutritional values are estimates and may vary based on specific ingredients used." }, "storage_and_reheating": { "room_temperature": "How long it safely keeps at room temperature and how to cover/store it", "refrigerator": "Store in airtight container for up to X days. Note how flavor or texture changes when served cold and whether to reheat or serve cold.", "freezer": "Freeze for up to X months. How to wrap properly, prevent freezer burn, thaw safely, and restore texture after freezing.", "reheating": "Specific reheating method (stovetop/microwave/oven), ideal temperature, time, what to add if needed (splash of liquid, etc.), and what to avoid", "make_ahead_strategy": "What steps can be done 1-2 days ahead, how to store the partially prepared dish, and what to do the day of serving" }, "faq": [ { "question": "A common question readers have about this specific recipe (troubleshooting, substitutions, timing, storage)", "answer": "80-120 words: specific, practical answer that genuinely solves the problem. Include the WHY, not just the WHAT. 8-10 FAQ entries required." }, { "question": "Another practical question about technique, ingredients, or results", "answer": "80-120 words: helpful, detailed answer based on real cooking knowledge" } ], "conclusion": "1-2 sentence warm closing. Invite readers to try the recipe and share results.", "seo": { "primary_keyword": "main 2-5 word search phrase for this specific recipe (e.g. 'southern pecan pie recipe')", "secondary_keywords": [ "related keyword phrase 1", "related keyword phrase 2", "how to make [recipe name]", "best [recipe name] recipe", "easy [recipe name]" ], "internal_links": [ {"text": "related recipe or technique text", "anchor": "anchor_slug_here"} ], "focus_keyword_density": "1.0-1.5%", "reading_level": "8th grade", "word_count_total": "approximately X words" } } TARGET AUDIENCE PROFILE: - American women, 55+ years old - Home cooks, not professional chefs - Cook for family, value simplicity - Prefer: classic comfort food, familiar ingredients - Avoid: trendy/exotic techniques, unusual ingredients - Tone: warm, practical, like advice from a neighbor CRITICAL AUTHENTICITY RULES: ✓ Use NATURAL language - write like a food blogger, not SEO copywriter ✓ Keyword density: 4-8 times MAXIMUM (not 10-15!) ✓ Imperfect is better - sound human, not AI ✓ Personal touches - "I love", "My family", "Works great when" ✓ Vary paragraph length - some 1 sentence, some 4 sentences ✓ Use contractions: "it's", "you'll", "don't" ✓ Occasional opinions: "I think", "in my experience" MANDATORY MINIMUM CONTENT PER FIELD (NON-NEGOTIABLE): ✓ "introduction": REQUIRED string, 250-350 words — NEVER null, NEVER omit this field ✓ "why_this_recipe_works": REQUIRED OBJECT (never a plain string!) with keys: - "section": section title string - "content": 400-600 words HTML string (3-4 paragraphs cooking science) - "ingredient_notes": object with 5-8 key ingredient names, each 70-100 words ✓ instructions: MINIMUM 9 steps, each step 100-150 words (include technique rationale, not just actions) ✓ pro_tips: 7-10 tips, each reason field 70-100 words of explanation ✓ common_mistakes_to_avoid: 5-7 mistakes, each fix 70-100 words ✓ variations: 5-7 variations, each description 80-110 words with technical_note ✓ storage_and_reheating: each field (room_temperature, refrigerator, freezer, reheating, make_ahead_strategy) 80-120 words ✓ faq: 8-10 Q&A pairs, each answer 80-120 words (troubleshooting, substitutions, timing) ✓ TOTAL: 4000-6000 words of actual content (ALL fields combined) NATURAL WRITING STYLE: ✓ First person occasionally: "I like to...", "I usually..." ✓ Second person casually: "You'll love...", "Try adding..." ✓ Mix sentence lengths ✓ Some shorter paragraphs (2 sentences) ✓ Some longer paragraphs (5 sentences) ✓ Transition words naturally: "Also,", "Plus,", "Another thing," ✓ Questions occasionally: "Want to make it spicier?" JSON VALIDATION (CRITICAL): ✓ Valid JSON only ✓ Double quotes for strings ✓ Escape quotes: \" ✓ Numbers without quotes: "prep_time": 15 ✓ No trailing commas ✓ Boolean: true/false (no quotes) ALLOWED HTML: ✓

    • ✗ No heading tags, div, span, or inline CSS INTELLIGENCE: ✓ Fill missing details logically ✓ Authentic techniques for cuisine type ✓ Realistic timing ✓ Appropriate suggestions ✓ Cultural accuracy Source Text: {SOURCE_TEXT}

Each title MUST start with one of: "Why", "What's the secret to", "[X] vs [Y]" (comparison), or "Are you still". 4. Title rules: max 80 characters, ends in "?", emotional tone, includes 1 Pinterest SEO keyword from the recipe name. 6. Description rules: 2–3 sentences, partially answers the question without fully revealing it (curiosity gap), ends with one clear CTA ("Save this", "Try it today", "Discover now", "Find out now"), 4–8 relevant hashtags placed at the very end, 2–3 emojis used naturally within the text (not stacked together at the end). 7. The "title" of pin_variations[0] must NOT be identical to the top-level "title" field — they serve different purposes (top-level title is the blog post title; pin_variations titles are all questions). NEVER leave a question or answer field blank, empty, or with placeholder whitespace. ✓ TOTAL: 4000-6000 words of actual content (ALL fields combined) NATURAL WRITING STYLE: ✓ First person occasionally: "I like to...", "I usually..." ✓ Second person casually: "You'll love...", "Try adding..." ✓ Mix sentence lengths ✓ Some shorter paragraphs (2 sentences) ✓ Some longer paragraphs (5 sentences) ✓ Transition words naturally: "Also,", "Plus,", "Another thing," ✓ Questions occasionally: "Want to make it spicier?" IMG_PROMPT_1:
A stunning ultra-premium food photograph of {title}, designed specifically to maximize visual craving, Pinterest saves, and instant scroll-stopping impact.

One hero portion of the dish dominates the foreground, positioned as the primary visual focal point. The most irresistible and appetite-triggering element of the recipe is prominently showcased, revealing rich textures, layers, freshness, and signature details that instantly communicate flavor and satisfaction.

Every visible ingredient is rendered with extraordinary realism and micro-detail. Crispy surfaces appear intensely crunchy, creamy elements look rich and velvety, sauces glisten naturally under the light, fresh ingredients appear vibrant and full of life, and layered components create visual depth and complexity.

Eye-level perspective, creating the sensation of sitting directly in front of the dish, ready for the first bite. The hero portion sits at the upper rule-of-thirds focal point while secondary elements softly recede into the scene, creating depth and visual storytelling.

Razor-sharp focus throughout the food. Every crumb, garnish, sauce streak, seasoning particle, texture variation, and surface detail remains tack-sharp and highly defined. Tiny realistic imperfections enhance authenticity and homemade appeal.

The food is elegantly plated on luxurious veined white marble. Warm directional side lighting sculpts every texture, creating appetizing highlights and dimensional shadows. Strong visual contrast between the dish and background ensures instant attention in a crowded Pinterest feed.

The food occupies most of the frame, emphasizing abundance and indulgence. The background melts into a creamy professional bokeh while the food remains perfectly sharp. No steam, no smoke, no fog, no clutter, no distracting props.

Photorealistic 8K detail. Editorial food magazine quality. Extreme texture realism. Rich appetizing colors. High dynamic range. Maximum craving appeal. Save-worthy Pinterest aesthetic. Scroll-stopping composition. Vertical 2:3 ratio. Slight Dutch tilt (2–3°). Luxury home kitchen atmosphere. Impossible to scroll past.
IMG_PROMPT_2:
An irresistible ACTION food photograph of {title}, captured at the exact serving moment — designed so it looks clearly DIFFERENT from the hero shot when both appear in the same Pinterest collage.

THE ACTION (pick the one that fits this dish): a serving spoon or fork lifting a generous portion with sauce slowly dripping, a cheese pull stretching, a ladle pouring, a knife revealing a clean cross-section with visible layers and steam-free molten interior, or a drizzle of sauce/honey/glaze falling onto the dish. Real human hand partially visible only if natural (clean, realistic skin texture, correct anatomy, five fingers).
